text: Or perhaps, ‘where the stores were kept’.
The words in brackets are probably due to the error of a copyist who wrote down a Hebrew word in verse 15 twice over.

The details in verses 29-32 are obscure, and it seems likely that the text has been badly handed down. After hearing that the Hebronites had charge of Israel west of Jordan, we do not expect to be told that they were registered east of Jordan, and held command there. It looks as if some other name, perhaps that of the Ozihelites (cf. verse 23 above) has dropped out. Nor is it easy to understand why Israel proper should have claimed the services of 1,700 Levites all told, if the tribes beyond Jordan were under 2,700 heads of Levite families.
